pageDescriptionAfter: Use our calculator to convert milligrams to milliliters by density or medication concentration — for medicine, syringes, and lab work.
inputsSubtitle: Enter your dose, pick a liquid or concentration, then calculate volume or mass instantly.
hintAmount: Enter the value you want to convert.
hintCustomDensity: Enter density in milligrams per milliliter for your substance.
hintMedication: If provided, this overrides the liquid preset. Parse from the bottle label.
importantBody: This calculator provides estimated volume conversions for planning only. Always verify medication doses with your pharmacist or doctor before administering. Concentrations vary by brand and formulation.
resultsSubtitle: Based on your inputs and the density or concentration used
emptyHint: Enter your amount and liquid details, then click “Calculate conversion” to see results here.
chartNote: Common doses at the density used in this calculation.
proTipsTitle: Pro tips for mg to mL conversions
proTip1: For water, divide mg by 1000 — e.g. 500 mg = 0.5 mL.
proTip2: Medicine labels often show mg per 5 mL — divide to get mg/mL first (250 mg/5 mL = 50 mg/mL).
proTip3: Never assume 1 mg = 1 mL; density always matters except for rough water estimates.
proTip4: Use a syringe marked in mL for liquid doses; double-check with your pharmacist.
proTip5: For lab work, confirm density at your working temperature — it can shift slightly.
errAmount: Enter a valid amount greater than zero.
errDensity: Enter a valid custom density greater than zero.
errMedication: Could not parse concentration — try formats like 50 mg/mL or 250 mg/5 mL.
